body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Body as process not substance — neuroplasticity reveals the self-model as continuously updated prediction.
**Phase context:** Neuroplasticity, body schema, perception plasticity

Paper: Rethinking the bodily self: evidence from the enfacement illusion in women at risk for eating disorders.
DOI: 10.1186/s40337-025-01461-1
Abstract: BACKGROUND: Bodily illusion research has demonstrated that altered bodily-self-perception in eating disorders (EDs) may be linked to abnormalities in the integration of sensory bodily signals. Experiencing bodily illusions can also temporarily reduce body image disturbance. Whether similar alterations in multisensory integration processes extend to self-face representation and whether face-based multisensory illusions can reduce face or body image disturbance remains unclear. This study investigated whether susceptibility to the enfacement illusion differs based on ED risk status and whether experiencing the illusion reduces face and body image disturbance. Bodily illusion research has demonstrated that altered bodily-self-perception in eating disorders (EDs) may be linked to abnormalities in the integration of sensory bodily signals. Experiencing bodily illusions can also temporarily reduce body image disturbance. Whether similar alterations in multisensory integration processes extend to self-face representation and whether face-based multisensory illusions can reduce face or body image disturbance remains unclear. This study investigated whether susceptibility to the enfacement illusion differs based on ED risk status and whether experiencing the illusion reduces face and body image disturbance.
